# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI CD Workflow
on: [push] # or [pull_request], depending on your configuration
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest   # specify a runner image here, e.g., 'ubuntu-latest' (or the name of another one) to use for this job run only that specific environment(s). If you want it in all environments at once then set up an array like [ubuntu-latest]
    steps: 
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2 # Check out your repository using `actions/checkout` action. This will clone the entire repo and put them into a new directory specified by you (default is '${{ github.workspace }}') as an example here, but it can be any other path
      - name: Set up Python 3.8 # Specify your own actions/setup-python action if not using community ones like above one to set the python version manually in a specific environment(s) or for all environments at once (e.g., [ubuntu-latest]) as an example here, but it can be any other name
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2 # Setup Python 3.8 using community action from above one with the version pinning specified in a specific environment(s) or for all environments at once (e.g., [ubuntu-latest]) as an example here, but it can be any other name
        with:   # Specify your own actions/setup-python settings if not using community ones like above one to set the python version manually in a specific environment(s) or for all environments at once (e.g., [ubuntu-latest]) as an example here, but it can be any other name
          python-version: 3.8 # Specify your own Python versions if not using community ones like above one to set the version manually in a specific environment(s) or for all environments at once (e.g., [ubuntu-latest]) as an example here, but it can be any other name
      - name: Install dependencies  // This step will install your project's requirements if they exist within this directory and its subdirectories using pip freeze > requirements/prod.txt command in the Python version specified above (e.g., python3.8) to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages, but it can be any other name
        run: | # Specify your own commands here if not running a specific one like actions setup-python or checkout action from community ones as an example  // This step will install all the dependencies in requirements/prod.txt file using pip freeze > requirements/prod.txt command to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip && \
          if [ -f requirements/prod.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements/prod.txt; fi  # This line will upgrade the 'pip' and also Install all dependencies from prod requirement file using command: `python3-tools` package to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages, but it can be any other name
      - name: Run tests // Specify your own commands here if not running a specific one like actions setup-python or checkout action from community ones as an example  This step will run all the test cases in Python using pytest command to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages, but it can be any other name
        run: | #Specify your tests runner commands here if not running a specific one like actions setup-python or checkout action from community ones as an example  // This step will execute all the test cases in Python using pytest command to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages, but it can be any other name
          python -m pytest # Add your tests runner commands here if not running a specific one like actions setup-python or checkout action from community ones as an example  // This step will execute all the test cases in Python using pytest command to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages, but it can be any other name
      - name: Deploy application   // Specify your own commands here if not running a specific one like actions setup-python or checkout action from community ones as an example  This step will deploy the app on Heroku using git push command to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages, but it can be any other name
        run: | #Specify your deployment commands here if not running a specific one like actions setup-python or checkout action from community ones as an example //This steps pushes all the code in current branch into Heroku using git push command to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages, but it can be any other name
          git push heroku master # Add your deployment commands here if not running a specific one like actions setup-python or checkout action from community ones as an example  // This steps pushes all the code in current branch into Heroku using git push command to ensure compatibility with different versions of packages, but it can be any other name
